text.title: growth-app-store-optimization Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for growth-app-store-optimization. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: Place primary keyword in app name (30 chars) and subtitle (30 chars) — these carry the highest weight in iOS ranking.
rule.rationale: Apple's algorithm weights title and subtitle fields most heavily. Keywords appearing here rank faster and higher than those only in the keywords field.

rule.statement: Use the 100-character iOS keywords field for secondary terms only: no spaces, comma-separated, no duplicates of name/subtitle, singular only (Apple auto-pluralizes).
rule.rationale: Duplicating name/subtitle terms in the keywords field wastes scarce characters. Apple pluralizes singular terms automatically, so adding plural forms is redundant.

rule.statement: Place primary keyword in the first sentence of the Google Play short description (80 chars) and repeat key terms 3–5 times naturally in the full description (4000 chars).
rule.rationale: Google Play indexes the full description as a web document. Keyword density and placement in the short description (shown in search results) directly affect ranking.

rule.statement: Research keywords from five sources before writing any metadata: competitor analysis, store autocomplete, review mining, related searches, and ASO tools (Sensor Tower, AppTweak, data.ai).
rule.rationale: Single-source research misses long-tail terms that competitors haven't targeted. Review mining surfaces the words actual users use — often different from marketing language.

rule.statement: Score every keyword candidate on relevance (0–10), volume (High/Med/Low), and difficulty (High/Med/Low) before prioritizing. Target high-relevance, medium-difficulty keywords first.
rule.rationale: High-volume keywords are dominated by top apps. Medium-difficulty keywords offer ranking opportunity while still delivering meaningful traffic.

rule.statement: Design icon with one focal element, no text, and brand colors. A/B test at least two variants before finalizing.
rule.rationale: Icons render at 29x29 pixels in some placements. Text becomes unreadable. Single-element icons are recognized faster in peripheral vision and crowded search results.

rule.statement: First two screenshots must show the core value proposition — assume most visitors never scroll to screenshot 3+.
rule.rationale: Eye-tracking studies show 60%+ of store visitors view only the first two screenshots. Burying the key feature in screenshot 4 costs conversion from the majority of visitors.

rule.statement: Add a benefit headline as text overlay on each screenshot. Show the app in active use, not empty states.
rule.rationale: Text overlay explains what the user sees. Empty states (blank dashboards, sign-up screens) communicate nothing about the product's value.

rule.statement: Use consistent visual style across all screenshots (same font, color palette, device frame).
rule.rationale: Inconsistent screenshots signal a low-quality or abandoned product and reduce trust.

rule.statement: Keep preview video to 15–30 seconds. Hook in first 3 seconds. Show real app usage only. No narration.
rule.rationale: Most users autoplay with sound off. Narration adds nothing. The first 3 seconds determine whether the viewer continues; starting with a menu screen or splash animation loses viewers immediately.

rule.statement: Trigger in-app review prompt only after a positive action (task completed, goal reached) and after the user's 3rd+ session. Never prompt on first launch or after negative events.
rule.rationale: Early prompts interrupt onboarding and elicit neutral/negative reviews from users who haven't experienced value. Post-positive-action timing captures peak satisfaction.

rule.statement: Use Apple's SKStoreReviewRequest API (iOS) or Google's In-App Review API (Android) — do not build custom review gates or incentivize reviews. Both are policy violations.
rule.rationale: Review gating (only showing the store prompt to users who said they liked the app) is banned on both platforms and can result in removal.

rule.statement: Respond to all negative reviews within 24 hours: acknowledge the issue, describe the fix or workaround, and ask the user to update their review if the issue is resolved.
rule.rationale: Public responses demonstrate support quality to prospective users scanning reviews. Users who see a thoughtful response are more likely to update their rating. App stores surface developer responses prominently.

rule.statement: Localize in this order: English (US/UK), Spanish (LATAM + Spain), Portuguese (Brazil), German (DACH), French, Japanese, Korean, Chinese (Simplified). Localize name, subtitle, description, screenshot text overlays, and keyword field for each locale.
rule.rationale: These locales represent the largest App Store revenue pools. Translating only the description while keeping English screenshots leaves 30–50% of conversion potential unrealized in non-English markets.
